children.label: A
children.text: audit State assessment systems and ensure that local educational agencies audit local assessments under subsection (e)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: execute the State plan under subsection (e)(3)(D); and

children.label: C
children.text: award subgrants under subsection (f); and

children.label: A
children.text: execute the State plan under subsection (e)(3)(D); and

children.label: B
children.text: award subgrants under subsection (f).

children.heading: Minimum amount
children.label: b Minimum amount
children.text: Each State that receives a grant under this section shall receive an annual grant amount of not less than $1,500,000.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: c Reallocation
children.text: If a State chooses not to apply for a grant under this section, the Secretary shall reallocate such grant amount to other States in accordance with the formula described in .section 6363(a)(4)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the audit the State will carry out under subsection (e)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: the stakeholder feedback the State will seek in designing such audit;
in the case of a State that is not receiving a grant under this section for the first time, the plan described in subsection (e)(3)(D); and
how the State will award subgrants to local educational agencies under subsection (f).

children.heading: Audits of State assessment systems and local assessments
children.label: e Audits of State assessment systems and local assessments
A
children.text: conduct a State assessment system audit as described in paragraph (3);

children.label: i
children.text: conducts an audit of local assessments administered by the local educational agency as described in paragraph (4); and

children.label: ii
children.text: submits the results of such audit to the State; and

children.label: C
children.text: report the results of each State and local educational agency audit conducted under subparagraphs (A) and (B), in a format that is widely accessible and publicly available.

children.heading: Resources for local educational agencies
children.label: 2 Resources for local educational agencies
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1)(B), each State shall provide local educational agencies with resources, such as guidelines and protocols, to assist in conducting and reporting audit results.

children.label: A
children.text: the schedule for the administration of all State assessments;

children.label: i
children.text: the purpose for which the assessment was designed and the purpose for which the assessment is used; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the legal authority for the administration of the assessment;

children.label: i
children.text: how teachers, principals, other school leaders, and administrators use assessment data to improve and differentiate instruction;

children.label: ii
children.text: the timing of release of assessment data;

children.label: iii
children.text: the extent to which assessment data is presented in an accessible and understandable format for all stakeholders;

children.label: iv
children.text: the opportunities, resources, and training teachers, principals, other school leaders, and administrators are given to review assessment results and make effective use of assessment data;

children.label: v
children.text: the distribution of technological resources and personnel necessary to administer assessments;

children.label: vi
children.text: the amount of time teachers spend on assessment preparation and administration;

children.label: vii
children.text: the assessments that administrators, teachers, principals, other school leaders, parents, and students, if appropriate, do and do not find useful; and

children.label: viii
children.text: other information as appropriate; and

children.label: i
children.text: eliminating any unnecessary assessments, which may include paying the cost associated with terminating procurement contracts;

children.label: ii
children.text: supporting the dissemination of best practices from local educational agencies or other States that have successfully improved assessment quality and efficiency to improve teaching and learning; and

children.label: iii
children.text: supporting local educational agencies or consortia of local educational agencies to carry out efforts to streamline local assessment systems and implement a regular process of review and evaluation of assessment use in local educational agencies.

children.heading: Local assessment description
children.label: 4 Local assessment description
children.text: An audit of local assessments conducted in accordance with paragraph (1)(B)(i) shall include the same information described in paragraph (3) that is required of a State audit, except that such information shall be included as applicable to the local educational agency and the local assessments.

children.heading: Subgrants to local educational agencies
children.label: f Subgrants to local educational agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State shall reserve not less than 20 percent of the grant funds awarded to the State under this section to make subgrants to local educational agencies in the State or consortia of such local educational agencies, based on demonstrated need in the agency’s or consortium’s application, to enable such agencies or consortia to improve assessment quality and use, and alignment, including, if applicable, alignment to the challenging State academic standards.

children.heading: Local educational agency application
children.label: 2 Local educational agency application
children.text: Each local educational agency, or consortium of local educational agencies, seeking a subgrant under this subsection shall submit an application to the State at such time, in such manner, and containing such other information as determined necessary by the State. The application shall include a description of the agency’s or consortium’s needs relating to the improvement of assessment quality, use, and alignment.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct an audit of local assessments under subsection (e)(1)(B)(i);

children.label: B
children.text: carry out the plan described in subsection (e)(3)(D) as it pertains to such agency or consortium;

children.label: C
children.text: improve assessment delivery systems and schedules, including by increasing access to technology and assessment proctors, where appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: hire instructional coaches, or promote teachers who may receive increased compensation to serve as instructional coaches, to support teachers in the development of classroom-based assessments, interpreting assessment data, and designing instruction;

children.label: E
children.text: provide for appropriate accommodations to maximize inclusion of children with disabilities and English learners participating in assessments; and

children.label: F
children.text: improve the capacity of teachers, principals, and other school leaders to disseminate assessment data in an accessible and understandable format for parents and families, including for children with disabilities and English learners.

children.heading: Local assessment
children.label: 1 Local assessment
children.text: The term “local assessment” means an academic assessment selected and carried out by a local educational agency that is separate from an assessment required under .section 6311(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: State
children.label: 2 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.
